title: oxalis frutescens subsp. borjensis
intro: Oxalis frutescens subsp. borjensis, also known as the Borja wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species within the Oxalis genus that is native to certain regions of South America. This plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory capabilities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Oxalis frutescens subsp. borjensis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. Oxalis frutescens subsp. borjensis can be prepared through various herbal methods, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
